Gravity is a fundamental force.<[silence:60ms]> It attracts two masses.<[silence:60ms]> Strength depends on mass and distance (Newton’s law:<[silence:80ms]> F = G  (m₁  m₂) / r²).<[silence:60ms]> G is the gravitational constant (6.674 × 10⁻¹¹ N·m²/kg²).<[silence:60ms]>

Einstein redefined gravity as spacetime curvature.<[silence:60ms]> Mass warps space;<[silence:80ms]> objects follow geodesics (shortest paths in curved space).<[silence:60ms]> Predicts light bending, confirmed during solar eclipses.<[silence:60ms]>

On Earth, gravity accelerates objects at 9.81 m/s² near the surface.<[silence:60ms]> Varies with altitude and latitude.<[silence:60ms]> Escape velocity:<[silence:80ms]> 11.2 km/s to break free from Earth’s pull.<[silence:60ms]>

Tides result from differential gravity—Moon’s pull stronger on near side, weaker on far side.<[silence:60ms]> Creates bulges in oceans and crust.<[silence:60ms]>

Black holes exhibit extreme gravity.<[silence:60ms]> Event horizon radius (Schwarzschild radius) = 2GM/c².<[silence:60ms]> Nothing escapes, not even light.<[silence:60ms]>

Dark matter (85% of universe’s mass) influences gravity but emits no light.<[silence:60ms]> Detected via galactic rotation curves and gravitational lensing.<[silence:60ms]>

Weakest of four fundamental forces (electromagnetism, strong/weak nuclear).<[silence:60ms]> Dominates at macroscopic scales due to infinite range and always-attractive nature.<[silence:60ms]> No known quantum theory reconciles gravity with other forces.<[silence:60ms]> Experiments seek graviton (hypothetical force carrier).<[silence:60ms]>
clean_story:
Gravity is a fundamental force. It attracts two masses. Strength depends on mass and distance (Newton’s law: F = G  (m₁  m₂) / r²). G is the gravitational constant (6.674 × 10⁻¹¹ N·m²/kg²).

Einstein redefined gravity as spacetime curvature. Mass warps space; objects follow geodesics (shortest paths in curved space). Predicts light bending, confirmed during solar eclipses.

On Earth, gravity accelerates objects at 9.81 m/s² near the surface. Varies with altitude and latitude. Escape velocity: 11.2 km/s to break free from Earth’s pull.

Tides result from differential gravity—Moon’s pull stronger on near side, weaker on far side. Creates bulges in oceans and crust.

Black holes exhibit extreme gravity. Event horizon radius (Schwarzschild radius) = 2GM/c². Nothing escapes, not even light.

Dark matter (85% of universe’s mass) influences gravity but emits no light. Detected via galactic rotation curves and gravitational lensing.

Weakest of four fundamental forces (electromagnetism, strong/weak nuclear). Dominates at macroscopic scales due to infinite range and always-attractive nature. No known quantum theory reconciles gravity with other forces. Experiments seek graviton (hypothetical force carrier).
